title: vatica thwaitesii
intro: Vatica thwaitesii, also known as the Sri Lankan ironwood or Sri Lankan walnut tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dipterocarpaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Vatica thwaitesii include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and providing antioxidant protection. The plant is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, to treat various ailments, and its bark and leaves are often used in traditional medicine in Sri Lanka.
